title: Overflow in Options
description: The parser returned a 'Header Overflow' error when trying to process this segment[cite: 9, 10, 11].

questions.question: Which field contains the fuzzed value causing the overflow exception[cite: 10]?

answer.explanation: The IHL (Internet Header Length) is the second nibble of the first byte[cite: 5, 6]. It is set to 0xF (15), which means the header should be 60 bytes (15 * 4). However, the Total Length field is 0x0040 (64 bytes), and the IP header options cannot exceed the boundary of the packet if additional protocol data is expected[cite: 10, 11].
